item.title: VP Duterte, complainants invited to March 25 House hearing on impeachment
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte has received an invitation to attend the March 25 hearing of the House Committee on Justice on the two impeachment complaints against her but has yet to decide on whether she will attend.

item.title: DSWD sets special relief payout day for QC tricycle drivers
item.description: The Department of Social Welfare and Development has announced a special payout on Friday, March 20, for qualified tricycle drivers in Quezon City who were not able to claim their P5,000 cash relief assistance under the Assistance to Individuals in Crisis Situation.

item.title: Over 300 Filipinos return to PH from UAE aboard third government-chartered flight
item.description: A total of 317 Filipinos has returned home from the United Arab Emirates Thursday afternoon aboard the third chartered flight arranged by the Philippine Government.

item.title: Defensor calls on Lacson to invite Bersamin, Olaivar, 18 ‘ex-Marines’ to next flood control hearing
item.description: Former congressman Michael Defensor said Senate President Pro Tempore Panfilo “Ping” Lacson, chairman of the Blue Ribbon Committee (BRC), should invite former Undersecretaries Adrian Bersamin and Trygve Olaivar to answer questions on the flood control scandal.

item.title: Defensor decries ‘double standard’ in Comelec decision clearing Marcoleta, ordering complaint vs donors
item.description: “Double standard” was how former lawmaker Michael Defensor described the Commission on Elections’ (Comelec) decision clearing Senator Rodante Marcoleta of an election violation, while ordering the filing of complaints against his campaign donors.

item.title: Rhian Ramos to pursue possible legal action over driver’s assault claims
item.description: Actress Rhian Ramos said she is already looking into possible legal actions against her former driver, who alleged that she, Michelle Dee, and Sam Panlilio physically assaulted and illegally detained him back in January.

item.title: ‘I am deeply sorry’: Suntay’s wife breaks silence on husband's Anne Curtis remark
item.description: The wife of Rep. Bong Suntay has issued a public statement distancing herself and her children from the controversial remarks the Quezon City lawmaker made during a House impeachment hearing, after his comments referencing actress Anne Curtis sparked widespread backlash online.

item.title: No double standard in impeachment proceedings — House justice panel members
item.description: Leaders of the House Committee on Justice asserted there was no double standard in its deliberations on the impeachment complaints against the country’s two highest officials.

item.title: DSWD urges stronger cooperation among ASEAN states amid global tensions
item.description: Social Welfare Secretary Rex Gatchalian called on other members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) for stronger cooperation amid global headwinds.

item.title: Man wanted for inciting to sedition surrenders to police
item.description: The Quezon City Police District on Thursday said it has successfully served a warrant of arrest against an individual wanted for inciting to sedition.
